ahp_elicited_weights.description: Primary weights, N=9 respondents, Harker (1987) correction applied to the two never-queried criterion pairs (Safety x Sustainability, Explainability x Sustainability). This is the empirical output of ahp_weight_elicitation.py run against the (anonymized, email-stripped) survey response data. Supersedes the N=6, default-1 vector used in the initial submission (see version_history below).

ahp_elicited_weights_default1_n9.description: N=9 respondents, but with the two never-queried cells left at the default value of 1 (the treatment used in the original N=6 script). Reported for comparison; not used in the main analysis. Mean CR is markedly worse than the Harker-corrected vector.

n6_default1_initial_submission.note: Original elicited vector from the first 6 respondents, missing cells defaulted to 1. Retained here for provenance and for the comparison discussed in the paper's revision history; no longer the primary vector.

baseline_illustrative_weights.description: Baseline weight vector used for the worked example in Section 4.1-4.4 of the paper. A rounded, illustrative approximation of the N=6 elicited vector, retained as the first and most conservative perturbation point in the Section 4.5 robustness analysis.

archetypes.description: Illustrative, hypothetical performance scores. NOT derived from disclosed data of any real manufacturer or system. See Section 4 disclaimer in the paper.

expert_panel_demographics.note: Recruited via non-probabilistic convenience sampling from the authors' professional networks. Three respondents were added after the initial submission; the panel grew from 6 to 9. E-mail addresses collected for coordination were deleted post-collection and are not part of this dataset.

dispersion_estimates.description: Two distinct quantities, both derived from the real N=9 individual response vectors -- see sensitivity_analysis.py for how each is used.

inter_respondent_relative_sd.description: Disagreement between individual experts (Harker-corrected individual weight vectors). NOT used directly in the Monte Carlo; reported as a measure of panel heterogeneity.

bootstrap_panel_mean_relative_sd.description: Standard error of the panel-mean weight estimate, obtained by bootstrap resampling (5,000 resamples with replacement of the 9 respondents, mean taken each draw). This is the quantity used to characterize uncertainty in the elicited weight vector for the Section 4.5 robustness check.
